cot_prefix: Okay, let's read through the passage and check the details for each of the options given. Option A: "In Thailand people give fish and birds to the birthday person as presents." Looking at the passage about Thailand, it says they buy live fish and birds for the birthday person and then the birthday person frees the animals. So this is correct. However, the passage might be more specific about other points. Option B: "In Holland people give presents to unbirthday persons on their birthdays." The passage says in Holland, children not only get presents but also give things out to classmates and teachers on their birthdays. So the birthday children give things to others, but it doesn't mention "unbirthday persons" as a specific term or description. Option C: "In Nigeria people who were born in the same period may celebrate their birthdays on the same day." The passage mentions that in Nigeria, people in a certain age group celebrate their birthdays together on a certain day. So this aligns with option C. Option D: "In Thailand a candle will be lighted on the birthday night." The passage says two tall candles are lighted the night before the birthday, not on the birthday night. So this is incorrect.
